title: Punch-Drunk Love
logline: A lonely, volatile small-business owner exploits a pudding promotion for airline miles while falling in love with his sister's co-worker, only to have a phone-sex extortion scheme threaten to destroy his chance at happiness.
antagonistOrConflict: Dean Trumbell and the phone-sex extortion scheme; Barry's own rage and isolation
tone: Anxious, tender, volatile, absurdist, emotionally raw
themes: The search for control in chaos

actBreaks.description: Barry and Lena's first date ends with him entering her apartment, marking his first genuine romantic connection and the shift from isolation to intimacy.

validation.ruling: Scene #30 is a quiet goodbye after the date, not a major turning point. Scene #28 (p59, 61%) better serves as Act 1 break: Barry's violent bathroom destruction is exposed when the manager confronts him, forcing payment or police—this public revelation of his rage issues and the threat of consequences marks an irreversible escalation that fundamentally shifts the story into higher-stakes territory.

actBreaks.description: Barry and Lena return from Hawaii to find the Stevens Brothers waiting in his garage, forcing the collision of his romantic hope with the violent consequences of his phone-sex mistake.

validation.ruling: Scene #70 (p87, 90%) functions as Act 2 break: Barry and Lena's intimate moment on the plane followed by the cut to black marks the end of their Hawaiian escape and the story's romantic peak before the final violent confrontation—the black screen signals a major structural division before the resolution phase begins.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality:
This script operates as a tightly-wound character study that uses linear progression to track Barry's emotional transformation. The structure is deceptively simple—boy meets girl, boy has problem, boy confronts problem—but Anderson layers it with behavioral specificity and tonal volatility. Act One establishes Barry's fractured world through accumulation: the mysterious harmonium, the pudding scheme, his sisters' harassment, the phone-sex call. These aren't traditional plot points but behavioral markers that reveal a man desperately seeking control through systems (airline miles, plungers) while unable to control his own rage.

The romance with Lena doesn't follow conventional beats—their connection builds through awkwardness and honesty rather than charm. Act Two becomes a pressure cooker as Barry's two worlds (the pudding scheme representing his need for order, the extortion representing chaos he's created) collide with his fragile new love. The Hawaii sequence (scenes 51-70) functions as both romantic climax and calm before the storm. Act Three is remarkably compressed—the confrontation and resolution happen swiftly once Barry finally channels his rage productively. The script trusts behavioral truth over plot mechanics.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing is deliberately uneven, mirroring Barry's psychological state. Act One moves in anxious bursts—short scenes, interrupted conversations, sudden violence (the harmonium drop at scene 1, the sliding glass door crash at scene 3, Barry's explosion at scene 11). The restaurant date (scenes 26-29) stretches time through discomfort, while the pudding-buying montage (scenes 44-48) accelerates into obsessive repetition. Hawaii (scenes 51-70) slows to allow genuine intimacy, creating the script's only sustained calm. The final act (scenes 71-83) rockets forward with single-minded intensity—Barry moves from victim to aggressor in just 12 scenes. The structure creates rhythm through contrast: manic energy, tender stillness, explosive release.

incitingIncident.description: Barry arrives at his sister Susan's house where his seven sisters remind him of when they called him gay and he threw a hammer through the sliding glass door while building a dog ramp.

validation.ruling: Scene #2 is too early (4%) and merely introduces Lena without disrupting Barry's world. Scene #10 (p19, 19%) is the true inciting incident: Barry calls the phone sex line, which sets in motion the extortion plot that becomes the main conflict driving the entire second half of the screenplay—this irreversible action shatters his status quo and introduces the stakes that will escalate throughout.

midpoint.description: Barry walks to his garbage area when Jim and Nate drive past in a Toyota pickup, David and Mike D confront him demanding money for the phone sex girl, Barry refuses, and they beat him and take his ATM card.

validation.ruling: Scene #36 (the mugging) occurs at 68%, too late for a midpoint and represents consequence rather than transformation. Scene #26 (p54, 56%) is the true midpoint: Barry's first real date with Lena at the restaurant marks his shift from reactive isolation to proactive emotional engagement—a point of no return where he commits to pursuing connection despite his fears, raising personal stakes significantly.

climax.description: Barry enters the mattress store, finds Dean getting his hair cut by Latisha in the back room, and threatens him calmly, saying he's a reasonable man but will hurt Dean if he or his brothers contact him or Lena again.

validation.ruling: Scene #80 (p94, 97%) is the climax: Barry confronts Dean directly in Utah, delivering his ultimatum about not hurting him or those he loves—this is the peak confrontation where Barry applies his character growth, resolves the extortion conflict, and demonstrates his transformation from passive victim to assertive protector.

scenes.heading: INT. WAREHOUSE - EARLY MORNING
scenes.summary: Barry Egan sits at his warehouse desk on the phone with an operator, meticulously questioning the details of a Healthy Choice airline miles promotion involving pudding purchases.

scenes.heading: INT. BARRY'S WAREHOUSE - THAT MOMENT
scenes.summary: Barry finishes a business call about plungers, walks outside to look at a harmonium in the alley, witnesses a car crash violently into a taxi, and a woman named Lena emerges asking to leave her car at his garage.
scenes.turningPointNote: Inciting incident: the harmonium appears and Lena enters Barry's life through the car crash

scenes.heading: INT. WAREHOUSE - OFFICE - EARLY MORNING
scenes.summary: Barry brings the harmonium into his office, tests it by pumping the bellows and playing notes, then is startled when Lance enters the warehouse.

scenes.heading: INT. WAREHOUSE/MAIN AREA - DAY
scenes.summary: Barry and Lance discuss plungers with customers while Barry's sister Rhonda calls to confirm he's coming to a party tonight, and Barry continues his sales pitch after the brief interruption.

scenes.heading: INT. WAREHOUSE - OFFICE - THAT MOMENT
scenes.summary: Barry's sister Kathleen calls to confirm he's coming to the party and repeatedly warns him not to be late or skip it like he usually does.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. WAREHOUSE - LATER (BRIDGE/BREAK MUSIC WISE)
scenes.summary: Barry and his workers have lunch by a parked lunch truck, Barry sees Lena's crashed car and asks a passing mechanic about it, then enters the warehouse.

scenes.heading: INT. WAREHOUSE/NEAR LANCE'S DESK - LATE AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Barry's sister Elizabeth visits the warehouse to invite him to meet her friend at tonight's party, Barry repeatedly refuses, and Elizabeth insists while revealing she's already told the friend about him.

scenes.heading: INT. WAREHOUSE - LATER/BLUE HOUR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Barry closes up alone, plays a few notes on the harmonium, then his face turns bright red as he holds back tears and has a brief burst of overwhelming emotion.

scenes.heading: INT. SUPERMARKET - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Barry shops at a supermarket, collides his cart with a six-year-old kid's cart, examines Healthy Choice products with airline mile stickers, and buys a birthday card.

scenes.heading: INT. SUSAN'S HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Barry arrives at his sister Susan's house where his seven sisters remind him of when they called him gay and he threw a hammer through the sliding glass door while building a dog ramp.

scenes.heading: INT. SUSAN'S HOUSE - HALLWAY - LATER
scenes.summary: Barry apologizes to Walter in a hallway and asks for help because he doesn't like the way he is sometimes, but Walter points out he's only a dentist.

scenes.heading: INT. BARRY'S APARTMENT - LATER
scenes.summary: Barry cuts out Healthy Choice coupons, notices a sexy ad for a phone sex line, calls the number, provides his credit card information, and asks for confidentiality before being transferred.
scenes.turningPointNote: Barry makes the phone sex call that will lead to the extortion plot

scenes.heading: INT. BARRY'S APARTMENT - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Barry receives the phone sex call from Georgia, gives his name as Jack, and they begin a conversation where she asks what he's doing and if he's watching pornography.

scenes.heading: INT. BARRY'S APARTMENT - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Georgia asks Barry sexual questions and he opens up about his business, saying he does pretty well but wants to make more money and diversify, then she asks him to stroke himself.

scenes.heading: INT. BARRY'S BATHROOM/BEDROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Barry washes his face in the bathroom, gets into bed in a t-shirt and underwear, and curls up in fetal position.

scenes.heading: INT. SAME EXACT ANGLE - MORNING
scenes.summary: Barry wakes up in the morning confused to find himself fully dressed in an Adidas sweat suit and sneakers.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Georgia calls Barry at home before work, thanks him for last night, and asks if he told her his real name, which he denies before she requests he send her money to help with her brother's medical bills.

scenes.heading: INT. BARRY'S WORK/WAREHOUSE - MORNING/LATER
scenes.summary: Barry arrives at work, explains the pudding promotion to Lance, plays the harmonium briefly, cuts up his credit card with scissors, receives four threatening calls demanding money, smashes his office with a golf club, then Lena arrives to pick up her car and asks him to dinner tomorrow.
scenes.turningPointNote: The extortion escalates with threatening calls and Barry's violent response, while Lena asks him on a date

scenes.heading: INT. WAREHOUSE - THAT MOMENT
scenes.summary: Lena asks Barry to dinner tomorrow, he agrees, she writes down her address and phone number, and Barry insists he didn't ask anyone for a shrink referral.

scenes.heading: INT. STEVENS BROTHERS HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Latisha convinces blonde brothers David and Jim to go collect money from Barry for Dean, mentioning Dean will loan them a car and that Barry is rich.

scenes.heading: INT. LOCAL BAR/PROVO - LATER
scenes.summary: Latisha, David, Jim and Nate meet Dean Trumbell at a bar where Dean agrees to pay two of them to go immediately, then Dean explodes in rage when a regular customer accidentally bumps into him.

scenes.heading: INT. LOCAL BAR/PROVO - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Dean and the brothers walk past a couple fighting violently, and Dean briefly intervenes to ask the man about booking a band.

scenes.heading: INT. DEAN'S OFFICE/UPSTAIRS/BACKROOM - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Dean takes the brothers to his upstairs office where a phone sex operator works, gives them Barry's information, refuses to cover their expenses, and threatens them when David protests.

scenes.heading: EXT. UTAH STREET/TRUCK/MOVING - DAY/MAGIC HOUR
scenes.summary: Nate, Jim and Dave drive down a Utah street and call their brother Mike D to invite him to Los Angeles.

scenes.heading: EXT. UTAH STREET/TRUCK/MOVING - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Mike D walks from work and gets in the car with his three brothers as they drive off toward Los Angeles, while in split screen Lena waits in her apartment and Barry arrives at her building.

scenes.heading: INT. RESTAURANT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Lena tells Barry at dinner that she saw his picture and came to drop her car off on purpose to meet him, and Barry shares his excitement about the pudding airline miles promotion.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Barry goes to the bathroom and violently destroys a stall and kicks apart the bathroom in a rage.

scenes.heading: INT. RESTARAUNT - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Barry returns to the table, the manager confronts him about destroying the bathroom, Barry denies it despite his bleeding hand, and the manager demands he pay for damages or he'll call the police.

scenes.heading: INT. BARRY'S CAR - DRIVING - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Barry and Lena drive in his car discussing the harmonium and the pudding, with Barry evasively answering her questions about traveling.

scenes.heading: INT. LENA'S APARTMENT - LATER
scenes.summary: Barry and Lena say goodnight at her apartment door, discussing when they'll see each other again as she mentions her upcoming trip to Hawaii.

scenes.heading: INT. RECEPTION AREA/LENA'S BUILDING
scenes.summary: Barry leaves Lena's building and the reception desk woman tells him there's a phone call for him, Lena tells him she wanted to kiss him, and he asks what to do about it.

scenes.heading: INT. MAZE OF CORRIDORS - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Barry walks through corridors looking for Lena's apartment, getting slightly lost while running and walking.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY - THAT MOMENT
scenes.summary: Barry finds Lena's door, they kiss and hug, he tells her he doesn't freak out very often despite what his sisters say, and they say goodbye.

scenes.heading: INT. BARRY'S APARTMENT/GARAGE
scenes.summary: Barry pulls his car into his garage and parks.

scenes.heading: INT. BARRY'S APARTMENT/KITCHEN - THAT MOMENT
scenes.summary: Barry enters his kitchen, turns on the light, and lifts a trash bag out of the trash can.

scenes.heading: EXT. BARRY'S APARTMENT - LATER
scenes.summary: Barry walks to his garbage area when Jim and Nate drive past in a Toyota pickup, David and Mike D confront him demanding money for the phone sex girl, Barry refuses, and they beat him and take his ATM card.
scenes.turningPointNote: The extortion becomes physical violence as the brothers attack Barry and force him to withdraw money

scenes.heading: EXT. GROCERY STORE/BANK PARKING LOT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Barry withdraws money from an ATM while the three brothers watch, David explains this is what happens when you're a pervert, and Barry protests he never promised to help anyone.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREETS & ALLEYS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Barry runs through streets and alleyways trying to escape.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET NEAR ATM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The brothers pull up alongside Barry in their truck as he runs down the street, Mike D asks where he's going, and Barry slows down and walks back in the opposite direction.

scenes.heading: INT. BARRY'S BEDROOM - NEXT MORNING
scenes.summary: Barry wakes up confused to find himself dressed in a full Lakers basketball outfit with a small bandage on his arm.

scenes.heading: INT. WAREHOUSE/BARRY'S OFFICE - MORNING
scenes.summary: Barry plays a few more notes on the harmonium that start to sound like a broken melody.

scenes.heading: INT. WAREHOUSE/BARRY'S OFFICE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Barry tells Lance he got in trouble from making a phone call, stares at the pudding, and receives a call from his sister Rhonda before he can explain further.

scenes.heading: EXT. WAREHOUSE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Barry and Lance exit the warehouse and get into Barry's car.

scenes.heading: INT. SMART AND FINAL - DAY
scenes.summary: Barry and Lance fill a shopping cart with pudding at Smart and Final while Barry explains the tremendous value of the promotion to Lance.

scenes.heading: INT. SMART AND FINAL/CHECK OUT - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Barry and Lance unload pudding packets at checkout, Barry insists each cup be scanned individually so it appears on the receipt, and the checkout girl questions what he's doing.

scenes.heading: EXT. SMART AND FINAL - DAY
scenes.summary: Barry and Lance push shopping carts to the car, Barry sees a Toyota pickup approaching and gets nervous, but it passes harmlessly, then Barry reacts emotionally to seeing a baby in a shopping cart.

scenes.heading: INT. SUPERMARKET - DAY
scenes.summary: Barry and Lance stack pudding in the car and Barry comments that it's funny you can get places in the world with pudding.

scenes.heading: EXT. PARKING LOT/ANOTHER SMART AND FINAL.
scenes.summary: Barry walks face-first into locked sliding doors at another Smart and Final, quickly returns to the car looking cautious, and tells Lance he thinks he has enough pudding.

scenes.heading: INT. BARRY'S WAREHOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Barry calls the airline promotion operator and learns his pudding order will take six to eight weeks to process, which conflicts with his plan to leave today.

scenes.heading: INT. WAREHOUSE AREA - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Barry tells Lance the pudding will take six to eight weeks to process so it won't work for today, puts Lance in charge, asks him not to tell his sisters anything, and leaves.
scenes.turningPointNote: Barry decides to leave immediately despite the pudding delay, setting up his impulsive trip

scenes.heading: INT. LOADING AREA/PLANE - DAY
scenes.summary: Camera follows Barry from ticket handoff through the corridor onto the plane.

scenes.heading: INT. PLANE - DAY
scenes.summary: Barry sits with a business guy on the plane and nervously admits he's never flown before as the plane begins to move.

scenes.heading: INT. PLANE - IN FLIGHT - LATER
scenes.summary: Barry watches a crying three-year-old child across the aisle and wells up with tears while smiling intensely, his face bright red and veins popping.

scenes.heading: INT. HAWAII AIRPORT - LATE AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Barry walks through the Hawaii airport looking at planes, palm trees, and people.

scenes.heading: EXT. AIRPORT/CURB - TAXI AREA - THAT MOMENT
scenes.summary: Barry gets into a taxi but realizes he doesn't know where to go and begins to contort and nearly snap emotionally.

scenes.heading: INT. WAKIKI STREET/PHONE BOOTH - LATER
scenes.summary: Barry calls Elizabeth from a phone booth asking about Lena's location in Hawaii, claiming Lena forgot her purse at his work.

scenes.heading: INT. ELIZABETH'S APARTMENT - THAT MOMENT
scenes.summary: Elizabeth confronts Barry about lying, calling him scared and a pussy, but he insists he just wants to know where Lena is staying.

scenes.heading: INT. WAKIKI STREET/PHONE BOOTH - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Barry calls the Sheraton Wakiki, gets connected to the wrong room with a man, calls back, reaches Lena, and tells her he's in Hawaii.
scenes.turningPointNote: Barry successfully reaches Lena in Hawaii, committing to pursuing her romantically despite his anxiety.

scenes.heading: INT. ROYAL HAWAIIAN/LOBBY - LATER
scenes.summary: Barry waits in the hotel lobby, sees Lena walking toward him with a smile, they awkwardly shake-hug-kiss and then start making out.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROYAL HAWAIIAN - PATIO/BAR AREA - MAGIC HOUR
scenes.summary: Barry and Lena sit at the Mai Tai bar on the beach, she thanks him for getting her out of her room, and they walk back toward the hotel holding hands.

scenes.heading: INT. ROYAL HAWAIIAN/HALLWAY - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Camera follows Barry and Lena down a hotel hallway as she reaches over and holds his hand.

scenes.heading: INT. ROYAL HAWAIIAN HOTEL ROOM - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Barry and Lena make out in bed, agree to have sex, and discuss an overwhelming urge to squeeze or bite things they find adorable, with Barry confessing he sometimes wants to hurt things he loves.

scenes.heading: INT. TOYOTA - MOVING - DAWN
scenes.summary: Mike D and Nate discuss in the car that Barry had more money to give up and suggest not telling Dean, deciding they're not too far to turn back.

scenes.heading: EXT. HIGHWAY - NIGHT (BLUE SKY - CRACK OF DAWN DEAL)
scenes.summary: The Toyota exits the freeway, goes under a bridge, and gets back on heading in the opposite direction.

scenes.heading: INT. HAWAIIAN HOTEL ROOM - NIGHT/NEAR DAWN - LATER
scenes.summary: Barry sleepwalks in the dark hotel room, talks gently to the wall, puts on a robe, and tells Lena he was talking to her last Thursday while still asleep.

scenes.heading: INT. KAHALA HOTEL - MORNING
scenes.summary: Lena talks to Elizabeth on the phone while Barry listens, lying that Barry never called her and agreeing he seemed strange, as they stare at each other across the room.

scenes.heading: EXT. TOYOTA - DRIVING - DAY (MELODY/SLIGHT)
scenes.summary: Camera follows the Stevens Brothers' car as they drive back.

scenes.heading: INT. KAHALA LOBBY - DAY (SCORE BACK TO RHYTHMIC)
scenes.summary: Barry and Lena walk out of the Kahala lobby to the valet.

scenes.heading: INT. HONOLULU AIRPORT/SECURITY - DAY (CONTD. UNTIL NOTED)
scenes.summary: Lena and Barry go through airport security.

scenes.heading: INT. AIRPORT/HONOLULU - AT THE GATE/WAITING AREA
scenes.summary: Barry and Lena sit side by side on the return flight, she asks if she can come home with him when they land, and he says he thought she was anyway.

scenes.heading: INT. BARRY'S GARAGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Barry and Lena pull into his garage when the Toyota rear-ends them, injuring Lena's head, and Barry goes violently after Mike D and Nate in a brawl.
scenes.turningPointNote: The extortion scheme escalates to direct violence as the Stevens Brothers attack Barry at his home, injuring Lena and triggering Barry's rage.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL - LATER
scenes.summary: Lena calmly gets a bandage applied at the hospital while a police officer questions her, and Barry backs away and walks out of the hospital.

scenes.heading: INT. BARRY'S WAREHOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Barry calls the phone sex line from his warehouse and demands to speak to the supervisor, telling Latisha he wants to talk to whoever runs this.

scenes.heading: INT. PROVO - BAR - BACK ROOM AREA - THAT MOMENT
scenes.summary: Latisha nervously puts Barry on hold and calls Dean, who tells her to put Barry through despite her protests.

scenes.heading: INT. DEAN'S MATTRESS/FURNITURE STORE - THAT MOMENT
scenes.summary: Dean takes Barry's call and they argue, with Dean threatening Barry's family and Barry demanding his money back before Dean hangs up on him.

scenes.heading: EXT. WAREHOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Barry runs down the alleyway with the phone in his hand, similar to how he ran with the harmonium at the beginning.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL/RECEPTION AREA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Barry returns to the hospital reception area looking for Lena but she's not there.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL/PAY PHONE
scenes.summary: Barry calls directory assistance from a hospital pay phone asking for the listing for D&D Mattress Man in Utah.
scenes.turningPointNote: Barry decides to confront Dean directly in Utah, taking active control of the conflict rather than remaining a victim.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET/MATTRESS STORE - DAY
scenes.summary: Barry arrives at Dean's furniture store in Utah.

scenes.heading: INT. MATTRESS STORE - DAY
scenes.summary: Barry enters the mattress store, finds Dean getting his hair cut by Latisha in the back room, and threatens him calmly, saying he's a reasonable man but will hurt Dean if he or his brothers contact him or Lena again.
scenes.turningPointNote: Barry directly confronts Dean face-to-face, asserting himself and drawing a clear boundary with a credible threat, marking his transformation from victim to agent.

scenes.heading: INT. WAREHOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Barry runs into his warehouse past Lance and the workers, tells them he's fine and had to go to Utah, and says he'll be right back.

scenes.heading: INT. LENA'S APARTMENT - CORRIDOR/VARIOUS
scenes.summary: Barry gets out of the elevator carrying the harmonium, makes the left turn this time, and runs down the hallway.

scenes.heading: INT. LENA'S APARTMENT/HALLWAY - THAT MOMENT
scenes.summary: Barry bursts through Lena's door with the harmonium, apologizes for leaving her at the hospital, confesses about the phone sex line and the blond brothers, and asks if she'll let him redeem his pudding miles to travel with her, and after she says he can't leave her like that, she invites him inside.
scenes.turningPointNote: Barry fully confesses his secrets to Lena and commits to being with her, and despite his abandonment she accepts him, resolving the central romantic conflict.
